prompt: Inside the Polymarket 'Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028' event (128 candidates), rank the top 12 candidates by 24-hour trading volume and chart each candidate's current YES probability — I want to see who the market is actively pricing vs who's just on the menu.
expectedReason: homepage showcase categorical charts need multi-row comparisons
responsePreview:
As of May 2, 2026, the Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028 market shows heavy trading activity around celebrity names and high-profile figures, despite most having very low individual win probabilities.

The following table ranks the top 12 candidates by 24-hour trading volume:

| Rank | Candidate | 24h Volume | YES Probability |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | Oprah Winfrey | $49,710,320.67 | 0.7% |
| 2 | Chelsea Clinton | $47,927,165.12 | 0.9% |
| 3 | Bernie Sanders | $45,330,764.92 | 0.5% |
| 4 | Andrew Yang | $45,192,320.79 | 0.7% |
| 5 | LeBron James | $40,915,979.60 | 0.8% |
| 6 | Hillary Clinton | $40,184,989.83 | 0.7% |
| 7 | George Clooney | $39,816,088.55 | 0.7% |
| 8 | Tim Walz | $39,483,318.48 | 0.7% |
| 9 | Kim Kardashian | $38,948,929.09 | 0.7% |
| 10 | Phil Murphy | $38,698,357.41 | 0.7% |
| 11 | Beto O’Rourke | $38,495,394.87 | 0.7% |
| 12 | MrBeast | $37,224,455.85 | 0.8% |

The accompanying chart highlights a significant gap between market activity and market expectations. While these 12 candidates are generating the most liquidity, their "YES" probabilities all remain under 1%. This suggests that while these names are frequently "on the menu" for speculative...
